content.problemStatement:
You are given a 000-indexed cyclic array AAA containing NNN non-negative integers. You can perform the following operation on this array :


Choose an index kkk, (0≤k<N0\leq k\lt N0≤k<N), and subtract 111 from both AkA_kAk​ and A(k+1) mod NA_{(k+1) \bmod N}A(k+1)modN​.

That is, subtract 111 from two adjacent elements of the cyclic array.
Determine if you can convert **all** elements of the array to 000 by performing the above operation several (possibly, zero) times.

You have TTT test cases to solve.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the length of the array.
The next line contains NNN space separated non-negative integers A0,A1,…,AN−1A_0, A_1, \ldots, A_{N-1}A0​,A1​,…,AN−1​, denoting the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing `YES` if it is possible to convert all the elements of AAA to 000, and `NO` otherwise.


Each letter of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, the strings `NO`, `no`, nO`, No` will all be treated as equivalent.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Perform the following operations:

Choose k=1k=1k=1 after which A = [1,1,2]A\:=\:[1,1,2]A=[1,1,2].
Choose k=2k=2k=2 after which A = [0,1,1]A\:=\:[0,1,1]A=[0,1,1].
Choose k=1k=1k=1 after which A = [0,0,0]A\:=\:[0,0,0]A=[0,0,0].

Test case 222: Perform the following operations:

Choose k=0k=0k=0 after which A = [0,2,4,2]A\:=\:[0,2,4,2]A=[0,2,4,2].
Choose k=1k=1k=1 after which A = [0,1,3,2]A\:=\:[0,1,3,2]A=[0,1,3,2].
Choose k=2k=2k=2 after which A = [0,1,2,1]A\:=\:[0,1,2,1]A=[0,1,2,1].
Choose k=2k=2k=2 after which A = [0,1,1,0]A\:=\:[0,1,1,0]A=[0,1,1,0].
Choose k=1k=1k=1 after which A = [0,0,0,0]A\:=\:[0,0,0,0]A=[0,0,0,0].

Test case 333: It can be proved that there does not exist any sequence of operations that will convert all the array elements to 000.
Test case 444: It can be proved that there exists a sequence of operations after which all elements of the array become 000.
